title: ACP Universal Frame Envelope
description: The standardized wire envelope used for all Agent Collaboration Protocol (ACP) communication across HTTP/QUIC/gRPC/WebSocket/P2P.

acp_version.description: Semantic version of the ACP specification, e.g., '1.0.0'.

message_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this frame (UUIDv4, ULID, or KSUID).

correlation_id.description: ID chaining requests, responses, streaming chunks, or workflow tasks together.

timestamp_ns.description: Epoch timestamp in nanoseconds when the frame was generated by the sender.

ttl_ms.description: Time-To-Live in milliseconds before this message is expired and dropped by routers/gateways.

agent_id.description: Decentralized Identifier (DID) or URI of the sending agent.

host_id.description: Identifier of the ACP Host / Runtime executing the sending agent.

instance_id.description: Specific instance or replica ID for stateless horizontal scaling.

pubkey.description: Base64 encoded public key (Ed25519/Secp256k1) used to verify this frame's signature.

target_id.description: Specific agent DID, topic string, service ID, or cluster endpoint.

routing_query.description: Dynamic skill/capability requirements if target_id is omitted (for skill-based discovery/routing).

reply_to.description: Alternative URI or broker topic for asynchronous response delivery.

router_path.description: Ordered list of router/gateway node IDs that processed and forwarded this frame.

auth_token.description: JWT / OIDC bearer token authenticating the sender entity.

capability_token.description: Zero-Trust signed Capability Token granting specific action rights.

nonce.description: Cryptographic random string ensuring replay protection when combined with timestamp.

signature.description: Base64 encoded cryptographic signature over canonical payload + headers.

encrypted_key.description: Base64 encoded ephemeral symmetric key encrypted for the target receiver public key (if payload is encrypted).

payload.description: The message body. Can be plaintext JSON or an encrypted envelope object containing iv, ciphertext, and tag.
